revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:гҒҲгӮ“гҒ¶гӮҠ]]
{{Otheruses|Festival in the southern region of Aomori Prefecture|й«ҳйҖҹгғҗгӮ№ operated by {{ill|KЕҚnan Bus Company|en|KЕҚnan Bus Company|qid=Q6455226|lt=Konan Bus|1=KЕҚnan Bus Company}}|Enburi-gЕҚ|qq=}} {{Special characters|qq=}} {{Event information | Event name = Enburi | English name = | image = Hachinohe Enburi 04A.jpg | image size = | Caption = People wearing the eboshi hats of Enburi | Type = {{ill|Festival|ja|зҘӯгӮҠ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҘӯ|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} | Nickname = | Formal name = | Former name = | Date = February | First held = | Last held = | Event time = | Venue = | Organizers = | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Partners = | Planning and production = | Su11ort = | Operation = | Producer = | Number of exhibitors = | Number of visitors = | Venue access name = | Nearest station = | Direct bus = | Parking = | URL = | Notes = }} '''Enburi''' (жңі) is a type of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing art|ja|дәҲзҘқиҠёиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku performing art|qq=}} widely held as a New Year ritual across {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|еҚ—йғЁең°ж–№ (йқ’жЈ®зңҢ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|1=Nanbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11408519}} centered around {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|qid=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe|1=Hachinohe}}, Aomori Prefecture. In Hachinohe City, the preservation organization is the Hachinohe Regional Enburi Federation Council. == Overview == Every year on the early morning of February 17th, over 30 "Enburi groups" perform a dedication at {{ill|ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|й•·иҖ…еұұж–°зҫ…зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}} in Hachinohe City. They then carry out a synchronized "Suri" performance in the city's {{ill|city center|ja|дёӯеҝғеёӮиЎ—ең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/city center|1=central city area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17174727}}, followed by visits to nearby areas for {{ill|Kadotsuke|ja|й–Җд»ҳгҒ‘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadotsuke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й–Җд»ҳ}} through February 20th. Performances are held at various locations in the city over four days<ref>[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/2 Agency for Cultural Affairs, "Hachinohe's Enburi" (National Cultural Heritage Database)] (Accessed June 28, 2010).</ref><ref>[http://www.city.hachinohe.aomori.jp/shiryo/guidebook/enburi.html Hachinohe City, "National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property - Hachinohe's Enburi"] (Accessed June 27, 2010).</ref><ref>[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/culture/minzoku_mukei/01.html Aomori Prefecture Cultural Property Protection Division, "Cultural Properties of Aomori - Hachinohe's Enburi"] (Accessed June 27, 2010).</ref><ref>[http://bunkashisan.ne.jp/search/ViewContent.php?from=11&ContentID=180 Foundation for Regional Creation, "Enburi" (Regional Cultural Asset Portal)] (Accessed June 28, 2010).</ref>. While it now has significant tourism a11eal, it originally served as a highly ritualistic type of {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|qid=Q3022455|lt=Dengaku|1=Dengaku}} (field dance). The "Jangi," a stick adorned with rattling plates or metal rings, used in the dance is said to originate from a farming tool called "Eburi," which was used to level rice fields, giving rise to the name "Enburi." The character "жңі" has traditionally been used, though it is read as "Enburi," not "Eburi." This unique reading reflects a regional dialect. == Enburi Dance == Dancers called "Tayu," dressed in large {{ill|Eboshi|ja|зғҸеёҪеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eboshi|qq=}} hats that depict the necks of horses or auspicious animals like {{ill|Cranes|ja|й¶ҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cranes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғ„гғ«|1=Crane (bird)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q25365}} or {{ill|Turtle|en|Turtle|qid=Q223044|lt=Turtles|1=Turtle}}, perform synchronized movements symbolizing rice planting. Groups typically consist of 20вҖ“30 people, including Tayus, musicians, singers, and dancers. The dance comprises "Starting Suri," "Middle Suri," and "Ending Suri," interspersed with various performances like "Matsunomai," "Ebisu Dance," "Daikoku Dance," and comical renditions of rice planting scenes called "Taue Manzai." == History and Development == Originally performed during the old {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|qid=Q13423452|lt=KoshЕҚgatsu|1=First Full Moon Festival}}, it was banned briefly after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} but was revived in 1881 as a ritual at ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine. In 1909, the festival date was aligned with the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} on February 17th<ref>[http://www.aomori-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja/sanpachi/jinja/18.htm Aomori Shrine Office, "ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine"] (Accessed June 28, 2010).</ref>. == Enburi Groups == As of 2024, 34 groups participate, including groups from {{ill|Hashikami|en|Hashikami|qid=Q1349010|lt=Hashikami Town|1=Hashikami}}, {{ill|Nanbu, Aomori|en|Nanbu, Aomori|qid=Q1206409|lt=Nanbu Town, Aomori|1=Nanbu, Aomori}}, and {{ill|Oirase|en|Oirase|qid=Q1349124|lt=Oirase Town|1=Oirase}}. Additionally, some elementary and middle schools organize "Children's Enburi Groups." == Cultural Significance == Recognized for its high cultural value as a Yoshuku performance, Hachinohe's Enburi was designated as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|йҒёжҠһз„ЎеҪўж°‘дҝ—ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иЁҳйҢІдҪңжҲҗзӯүгҒ®жҺӘзҪ®гӮ’и¬ӣгҒҡгҒ№гҒҚз„ЎеҪўгҒ®ж°‘дҝ—ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ}} in 1971 and as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|йҮҚиҰҒз„ЎеҪўж°‘дҝ—ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}} in 1979. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == See also == * {{ill|Traditional performing arts|ja|дјқзөұиҠёиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Traditional performing arts|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®дјқзөұиҠёиғҪ|1=Theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku performing arts|ja|дәҲзҘқиҠёиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku performing arts|qq=}} == External links == * [https://visithachinohe.com/stories/enburi-info/ "What is Enburi?"] - VISIT HACHINOHE * [http://machi.hi-net.ne.jp/enburi/index.html Enburi of Hachinohe - Machi no Eki Hachinohe] * {{Cultural Asset Portal|02_aomori|6968|qq=}} {{Cultural heritage of Japan|qq=}}
{{otheruses|Aomori Prefecture's southern region festival|the highway bus operated by [[Konan Bus]]|Enburi-go}} {{Special characters}} {{Event information | Event Name = Enburi | English Name = | image = Hachinohe Enburi 04A.jpg | image Size = | Caption = People wearing eboshi (headgear) during Enburi | Type = [[Festival]] | Nickname = | Official Name = | Former Name = | Date = February | First Held = | Last Held = | Time = | Venue = | Organized by = | Co-organizers = | Sponsors = | Supporters = | Concept/Production = | Cooperation = | Management = | Producer = | Number of Exhibitors = | Visitors = | Venue Access = | Nearest Station = | Direct Bus = | Parking = | URL = | Notes = }}
'''Enburi''' (жңі) is a form of {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku-geino|ja|дәҲзҘқиҠёиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yoshuku-geino|qq=}} (ritual performance for agricultural blessings) traditionally practiced as a spring ritual in {{ill|Hachinohe|en|Hachinohe|qid=Q191914|lt=Hachinohe|1=Hachinohe}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, and surrounding areas in the {{ill|Nanbu Region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|еҚ—йғЁең°ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu Region (Aomori Prefecture)|1=Nanbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11408517}}. Preservation of the tradition in Hachinohe is overseen by the Hachinohe Regional Enburi Federation Council.
== Overview == Every year, beginning early in the morning on {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|qid=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}, over 30 "Enburi groups" (жңізө„) gather at {{ill|ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine|ja|й•·иҖ…еұұж–°зҫ…зҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine|qq=}} in Hachinohe to make ritual offerings. Following this, a grand performance called "issai-suri" takes place in the city center. Subsequently, the groups visit nearby communities as {{ill|door-to-door performers|ja|й–Җд»ҳгҒ‘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/door-to-door performers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й–Җд»ҳ|1=Kadotsuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11654943}}, concluding their performances on {{ill|February 20|en|February 20|qid=Q2344|lt=February 20|1=February 20}}. Enburi has evolved to include strong tourism elements but originates as a ritualistic {{ill|Dengaku|en|Dengaku|qid=Q3022455|lt=dengaku|1=Dengaku}} (traditional agricultural dance) closely related to {{ill|taue-odori|ja|з”°жӨҚиёҠ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/taue-odori|1=Rice-planting dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11577057}}.
Performers use a pole known as a "jangi," which is fitted with components like naruko-ita (clapper plates) or metal rings. This tool symbolizes {{ill|eburi|ja|гҒҲгҒ¶гӮҠ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eburi|1=eburi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125750529}}, a traditional agricultural tool used to level fields before rice planting. The dance, therefore, derives its name from this agricultural implement. The kanji "жңі" represents this tradition and is uniquely read as "enburi," reflecting the region's distinct dialect and interpretation.
== The Enburi Dance == The Enburi performance features dancers wearing large, elaborate {{ill|eboshi|ja|зғҸеёҪеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/eboshi|qq=}} headgear shaped like a horse's neck and adorned with motifs such as cranes or turtles. These dancers, known as "tayu" (еӨӘеӨ«), move rhythmically, tilting their heads and striking the ground with their jangi. The performance mimics agricultural activities and is referred to as "suru" (ж‘әгӮӢ, meaning to rub or strike the ground). Each Enburi group comprises 20вҖ“30 members, including dancers, musicians, and singers.
The dance sequence consists of "suri-hajime" (ж‘әгӮҠе§ӢгӮҒ, opening), "naka no suri" (дёӯгҒ®ж‘әгӮҠ, middle), and "suri-osame" (ж‘әгӮҠзҙҚгӮҒ, conclusion). Between these sequences, various accompanying performances are staged, including children's "Matsu no Mai" (жқҫгҒ®иҲһ, Pine Dance) and comedic acts such as "Taue Manzai" (з”°жӨҚдёҮжүҚ, comedic rice planting).
There are two main styles of Enburi: 1. '''Naga-Enburi''' (гҒӘгҒҢгҒҲгӮ“гҒ¶гӮҠ): A slower, more ritualistic style featuring the "goiwai-uta" (ritual blessing song) and sacred sake offerings. 2. '''Dosai-Enburi''' (гҒ©гҒҶгҒ•гҒ„гҒҲгӮ“гҒ¶гӮҠ): A faster-paced style marked by colorful tassels (maegami) on the headgear. The leader, called "TЕҚkurЕҚ" (и—Өд№қйғҺ), wears a unique headpiece adorned with flowers like peonies or deutzia.
== History == Originally performed during the {{ill|First Full Moon Festival|en|First Full Moon Festival|qid=Q13423452|lt=Koshogatsu|1=First Full Moon Festival}} (Little New Year), Enburi was temporarily banned after the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}} due to its resemblance to begging. In {{ill|1881|en|1881|qid=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}}, local leaders like {{ill|Ozawa Tamon|ja|еӨ§жҫӨеӨҡй–Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozawa Tamon|1=Osawa Tamon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11437626}} revived it as a ritual of ChЕҚjasan Shinra Shrine. By {{ill|1909|en|1909|qid=Q2057|lt=1909|1=1909}}, its schedule aligned with the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Prayer Festival|1=Kinensai}} at {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} on February 17.
== Enburi Groups == In addition to 34 groups in Hachinohe as of {{ill|2024|en|2024|qid=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}}, there are three groups each in {{ill|Hashikami|en|Hashikami|qid=Q1349010|lt=Hashikami|1=Hashikami}} and {{ill|Nanbu, Aomori|ja|еҚ—йғЁз”ә|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu, Aomori|1=NambuchЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q126073891}}, and one group in {{ill|Oirase|en|Oirase|qid=Q1349124|lt=Oirase, Aomori|1=Oirase}}. Schools in these areas also participate through extracurricular and club activities, forming "Children's Enburi Groups."
== Cultural Heritage == In recognition of its historical and cultural value, Enburi was selected as a {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|lt=Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|йҒёжҠһз„ЎеҪўж°‘дҝ—ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иЁҳйҢІдҪңжҲҗзӯүгҒ®жҺӘзҪ®гӮ’и¬ӣгҒҡгҒ№гҒҚз„ЎеҪўгҒ®ж°‘дҝ—ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ}} in {{ill|1971|en|1971|qid=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} and designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|йҮҚиҰҒз„ЎеҪўж°‘дҝ—ж–ҮеҢ–иІЎ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Japan)|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}} on {{ill|February 3, 1979|ja|1979th year 2жңҲ3ж—Ҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/February 3, 1979|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Theatre of Japan|lt=Theatre of Japan|lt=Traditional performing arts of Japan|ja|дјқзөұиҠёиғҪ|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Theatre of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®дјқзөұиҠёиғҪ|1=Theatre of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1364447}} * {{ill|Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku|lt=Yoshuku-geino|ja|дәҲзҘқиҠёиғҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshuku-geino|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://visithachinohe.com/stories/enburi-info/ "What is Enburi?"] - VISIT HACHINOHE * [http://machi.hi-net.ne.jp/enburi/index.html Enburi in Hachinohe] * {{Regional cultural asset portal|02_aomori|6968}} {{Cultural Properties of Japan}}
}}
